cta.title: Not Sure Whether Our Care Is Right for You?
cta.description: Contact us to arrange a complimentary consultation and discuss your individual health concerns with our team.

hero.title: Conditions We Help Support
hero.subtitle: From commuter neck tension to stubborn fatigue, we map patterns with classic diagnostics and modern safety—right here in Great Neck.

categories.name: Pain Management
categories.subtitle: Support for flare-ups and nagging pain without defaulting to meds first
categories.description: Desk hours, yard work, and winter weather on the North Shore show up as spasms, sciatic pull, or stiff necks. We use needles, cupping, or bodywork depending on what your exam suggests.

categories.name: Emotional & Mental Wellbeing
categories.description: Calmer stress chemistry, steadier sleep, and clearer focus when life feels dialed to eleven

categories.name: Digestive Health
categories.description: Mealtime discomfort, reflux, or IBS-type swings once serious causes are ruled out with your doctor

categories.name: Women's Health
categories.description: Cycles, perimenopause, and fertility prep coordinated honestly with your OB or RE team

categories.name: Respiratory Issues
categories.description: Allergens, lingering coughs, and tight chest days—we add support, not a substitute for inhalers you already rely on

categories.name: Immune System
categories.description: You catch everything on the LIRR or bounce back slowly—TCM can strengthen wei qi between seasons

conditions.title: Lower Back, Upper Back & Sciatica Pain
conditions.description: Sacroiliac irritation, disc-related ache, or tight paraspinals—we treat the pattern, whether it started yesterday or years ago.
conditions.tcmApproach: We look for stagnation or cold lodged along the bladder and gallbladder channels, then mix needle depth, cupping drag, or warming moxa depending on what palpation shows.

conditions.title: Pain in the Neck & Shoulders
conditions.description: Tech-neck, bag-carrying shoulders, or post-whiplash tightness—common Great Neck complaints after long drives and laptop marathons.
conditions.tcmApproach: Yangming and taiyang channel segments often respond to gentle gua sha plus acupuncture; we avoid brute-force torque on vulnerable disks.

conditions.title: Joint Pain & Arthritis
symptoms: Pain that worsens when the weather changes
conditions.description: Weather-sensitive knees, stiff hands, or inflammatory flares—supportive care alongside rheumatology plans you already have.
conditions.tcmApproach: Bi patterns get sorted into wind-cold-damp or heat; moxa helps cold types, while clearing herbs anchor hot, swollen joints when appropriate.

conditions.title: Migraines & Headaches
conditions.description: Band-like tension, one-sided migraines, or cervicogenic pain after screen time.
conditions.tcmApproach: Different headaches need different angles—ascending yang, blood deficiency, or neck referral. We pair scalp and distal points, then teach triggers you can actually control.

conditions.title: Anxiety & Panic Disorder
conditions.description: Racing mind before bed, shallow breathing, or panic spikes that labs can’t explain—supportive care, not a replacement for therapy or meds you need.
conditions.tcmApproach: Heart-kidney disconnect and stuck liver qi show up differently on pulse; we anchor shen with gentle points and breath cues you can reuse at home.

conditions.title: Depression & Persistent Low Mood
symptoms: Difficulty with focus and clear thinking
conditions.description: Flat mood, heavy limbs, or missing motivation after prolonged stress—coordinated with counseling or psychiatry when you’re already in care.
conditions.tcmApproach: We unblock liver qi constraints, build blood if you’re depleted, and strengthen digestion when gut-brain loops drag you down.

conditions.title: Insomnia & Sleep Problems
conditions.description: Can’t wind down, wake at 3 a.m., or sleep but never feel rested—patterns we map instead of forcing blanket sedatives.
conditions.tcmApproach: Yin-deficient heat versus gallbladder timidity needs opposite strategies; herbs and points follow the nightly story you describe, not a generic ‘insomnia script’.

conditions.title: Stress and Burnout
conditions.description: Deadline fatigue, short fuse, or that ‘always on’ hum even on weekends.
conditions.tcmApproach: We smooth constrained liver qi, replenish spleen qi drained by worry, and stitch in micro-habits so treatment holds between visits.

conditions.title: IBS and Digestive Issues
conditions.description: Cramping, bloating, or urgent bowels after stressful weeks—after your GI clears red flags.
conditions.tcmApproach: Liver overacting on spleen is the classic IBS pair; we calm peristalsis with points, then settle gut transit with herbs if needed.

conditions.title: Acid Reflux and GERD
conditions.description: Burning chest after late meals or morning sour taste—adjunct care while you follow GI guidance.
conditions.tcmApproach: Rebellious stomach qi plus phlegm-heat calls for descending herbs and points along ren mai and stomach channel—not endless acid suppression alone.

conditions.title: Chronic Constipation
symptoms: Bowel movements that are infrequent or irregular
conditions.description: Hard stools, incomplete evacuation, or reliance on stimulant laxatives you’d rather reduce.
conditions.tcmApproach: Qi stagnation vs blood dryness vs yang deficiency move bowels differently—classical formulas support peristalsis without harsh cascara defaults.

conditions.title: Fertility and Conception Support
conditions.description: Cycle mapping, luteal support, or sperm parameter tweaks—while you stay connected with your OB or fertility clinic.
conditions.tcmApproach: Kidney-chong-ren channels guide timing; we sync herbs and needles to follicular vs luteal phases and IVF freeze cycles when labs allow.

conditions.title: Menstrual Concerns
conditions.description: Crampy flows, clotty bleeding, or emotionally rough luteal weeks.
conditions.tcmApproach: Cold stagnation vs qi constraint needs warmth or movement strategies; basal temp charts help us time interventions honestly.

conditions.title: Menopause-Related Symptoms
conditions.description: Heat rushing, sweaty nights, or mood volatility during hormone shifts.
conditions.tcmApproach: Yin-nourishing anchoring clears empty heat; jittery shen settles with heart-kidney water adjustments rather than one-size smoothies.

conditions.title: PCOS and Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
symptoms: Irregular menstrual cycles or no menstruation
conditions.description: Irregular ovulation, insulin-resistant weight creep, or stubborn androgen acne patterns.
conditions.tcmApproach: Damp phlegm in the lower burner meets liver qi knots—herbs transform phlegm while acupuncture nudges ovulatory timing transparently.

conditions.title: Seasonal Allergies and Hay Fever
conditions.description: Nassau County pollen waves or dusty subway commutes leaving you foggy and congested.
conditions.tcmApproach: Fortify lung wei qi before peak season when possible; acute attacks get diffusing herbs instead of only stacking antihistamines.

conditions.title: Asthma Support
symptoms: Symptoms brought on by physical activity
conditions.description: Chest tightness or wheeze between inhaler checkups—never a substitute for rescue meds you’re prescribed.
conditions.tcmApproach: Kidney grasping qi deficiency plus lingering phlegm guides whether we emphasize root tonification or bronchial clearing first.

conditions.title: Persistent Chronic Cough
conditions.description: Dry tickle, phlegm that won’t clear, or post-viral cough once imaging and strep are sorted.
conditions.tcmApproach: Lung yin dryness, stubborn phlegm heat, or throat wei qi irritation each steer different point pairs and formulas.

conditions.title: Recurring Colds & Flu
conditions.description: You’re first in the house to get every bug or bounce back slowly after a cold.
conditions.tcmApproach: Wei qi drills combine acupuncture, moxa, and spleen-friendly diet tweaks so defensive qi rebuilds between school seasons.

conditions.title: Persistent Fatigue
symptoms: Finding it hard to manage everyday activities
conditions.description: Heavy legs, brain fog, or unrefreshing sleep once thyroid and iron panels are reviewed with your physician.
conditions.tcmApproach: Spleen qi collapse vs kidney yang fatigue vs blood dryness—the mix of tonification and gentle movement differs, so we don’t default to stimulants.
treatmentMethods: Guidance for balancing rest with physical activity

introduction.note: Don’t see your concern listed? Call—many issues slot into the patterns below even if the name differs.
introduction.text: These are the clusters Dr. Gang Shi addresses most often from our Great Neck treatment rooms. Listing isn’t a promise of cure; it’s a map of where classical medicine still pairs well with today’s diagnoses.
